clues.question: In 1957, rival teenage gangs sang "Tonight" in this legendary New York musical, a landmark of modern theater with a soaring Bernstein score and unforgettable choreography.
Often called the city that never sleeps, New York is home to this fictional stage work about Tony and Maria, a tragic love story set amid a gang war on the streets.
Named for a rough working-class neighborhood on Manhattan's rough west side, this fictional musical centers on a deadly street feud between two rival teenage street gangs.
Around the time Leonard Bernstein was reshaping Broadway with electric scores, this invented title might have fit a drama of finger snaps and rumbling gangs.
Judy Kuhn never starred in this fabricated show, but its title sounds like a cousin to the great Broadway tragedies set beneath the city's iconic fire escapes.
